title: IdentityProviderSettings - Metadata API
description: Represents the settings used to enable or disable Salesforce as a SAML identity provider for single sign-on (SSO).

certificateName.description: The certificate that Salesforce uses to communicate with SAML SSO service providers, such as third-party service providers or another Salesforce org acting as a service provider. You can enter the name of a self-signed certificate or a certificate signed by a certificate authority.

enableIdentityProvider.description: Indicates whether Salesforce can be used as a SAML identity provider (true) or not (false).

wsdl_segment:
```xml
<xsd:complexType name="IdentityProviderSettings">
  <xsd:complexContent>
    <xsd:extension base="tns:Metadata">
      <xsd:sequence>
        <xsd:element name="certificateName" type="xsd:string"/>
        <xsd:element name="enableIdentityProvider" type="xsd:boolean"/>
      </xsd:sequence>
    </xsd:extension>
  </xsd:complexContent>
</xsd:complexType>
```

declarative_metadata_sample_definition.description: The following is an example of an IdentityProviderSettings component. In this example, Salesforce is enabled as a SAML identity provider.
